question: If this whole pint is made up of ice cream, how many percent above or below the US federal standards for butterfat content is it when using the standards as reported by Wikipedia in 2020? Answer as + or - a number rounded to one decimal place.

working_plan: Extract the relevant data from the artifact.
Use sandboxed code for any calculations or parsing.

citations: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ice_cream - US FDA rules requiring minimum 10% milk fat for ice cream
solver.reasoning_summary: Calculated butterfat content from nutrition facts (62g fat / 432g total weight = 14.35%), compared to US federal standard of 10% minimum milk fat from Wikipedia, resulting in +4.4% difference when rounded to one decimal place.

Annotator Metadata.Steps:
1. Open the image.
2. Search "butterfat wikipedia" on Google search.
3. Open the Butterfat Wikipedia page.
4. Click "View history" on the page.
5. Scroll down to the end of 2020 and click the last 2020 version of the page.
6. Check the ice cream requirement for fat content (10%).
7. Click "View history" on the page.
8. Scroll down to the beginning of 2020 and click the last 2019 version of the page.
9. Check the ice cream requirement for fat content to ensure it's the same (10%).
10. Calculate the fat percentage of the pint of ice cream from the image of the nutrition panel (21g fat per serving / 144g ice cream per serving = 14.6%).
11. Calculate the difference from the standard (14.6% - 10% = 4.6%).
Annotator Metadata.Tools:
1. Image recognition tools
2. Calculator
3. Web browser
4. Search engine
